subject: In this issue: Nominations open for the BioMed Central Research Awards

body_html:
To view the email in your web browser go to: http://www.biomedcentral.com/info/update

Advertisement

 
Thursday 31 July 2008

BioMed Central recently announced that nominations for the 3rd Annual Research Awards are now open. The awards, now in their third year and growing in popularity, recognize excellence in research that has been made universally accessible by open access publication.
Read More


Scandinavian Journal of Trauma, Resuscitation and Emergency Medicine moves to BioMed Central
Editor-in-Chief Hans Morten Lossius
 

SJTREM, the official journal of The Scandinavian Networking Group on Trauma and Emergency Management, provides a high-quality forum for all health professionals involved in pre- and in-hospital emergency medicine, critical care and trauma management.
Read More
BioData Mining launches
Editors-in-Chief: Jesus Aguilar-Ruiz and Jason H. Moore
 

BioData Mining is a new independent journal encompassing research on data mining applied to high-dimensional biological and biomedical data, focusing on computational aspects of knowledge discovery from large-scale genetic, transcriptomic, genomic, proteomic, and metabolomic data.
Read More

New supplements
  Diagnostic Pathology has published 'New trends in digital pathology: Proceedings of the 9th European Congress on Telepathology and 3rd International Congress on Virtual Microscopy' from the meeting held in Toledo, Spain, 15-17 May 2008.
 
  BMC Endocrine Disorders has published 'Cross-sectional study of height and weight in the population of Andalusia from age 3 to adulthood' edited by Maximino Fernández Pérez.
 
  BMC Bioinformatics has published 'Selected proceedings of the Fifth International Workshop in Data Integration in the Life Sciences 2008' from the meeting held in Evry, France, 25-27 June 2008.

 New from Journal of Biology

A minireview by Nico van Straalen and Dick Roelofs describes how environmental pollution can be monitored by combining metabolomics and transcriptomics approaches. The article discusses a systems ecotoxicology study in earthworms by Peter Kille and colleagues, recently published in BMC Biology.

  Genomics technology for assessing soil pollution
Nico M van Straalen and Dick Roelofs
Journal of Biology 2008, 7:19

If you wish to be notified when new articles are published in Journal of Biology, please sign up for article alerts and/or RSS feeds.

 

European law lacks deferred consent
Trials 2008, 9:45
An update of European regulations to enable implementation of the emergency trial consent model has been recommended, following a survey of more than 200 Polish physicians’ informed consent practices.

     
  Genomic perspectives on butterfly wing mimicry
BMC Genomics 2008, 9:345
High sequence homology, gene order conservation and microsynteny are detected in regions linked to three different loci that govern the mimetic wing color patterns in two related species of butterflies, Heliconius erato and H. melpomene.
     

Review
High-mobility group box protein 1 (HMGB1): an alarmin mediating the pathogenesis of rheumatic disease
David S Pisetsky, Helena Erlandsson-Harris, Ulf Andersson
Arthritis Research & Therapy 2008, 10:209 (30 June 2008)

  Recommend Arthritis Research & Therapy to your library

Over 4000 top researchers identifying major advances, including some you may have missed.

   

"The paper reports that influenza virus, like most enveloped viruses, incorporates host proteins during budding." by Rino Rappuoli (Chiron Corporation, Italy).
  Read the Full Evaluation

Not a subscriber? You can register for a free trial or recommend Faculty of 1000 Biology to your librarian.
 

"I recommend that every cancer patient is evaluated about whether or not there has been a loss of interest or pleasure in doing things. If there is a problem with anhedonia (or depressed mood), further evaluation is needed before recommending a specific course of action." by Michael Fisch (University of Texas, M.D. Anderson Cancer Center, United States of America).
  Read the Full Evaluation

Not a subscriber? You can register for a free trial or recommend Faculty of 1000 Medicine to your librarian.

E-cadherin expression in the mouse yolk sac.

BMC Developmental Biology 2008, 8:65


Fatal injection of ranitidine

A 51-year-old man died suddenly after receiving 50mg of intravenous ranitidine (Zantac®) for routine treatment of his stress ulcer.

Journal of Medical Case Reports 2008, 2:232

Sensory Evaluation Analyst
Phillip Morris Products
Neuchatel CHE
